channel.title: dotnet/csharplang Pull Requests
channel.description: Pull Requests from https://github.com/dotnet/csharplang, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: PR: Call EnsureSufficientExecutionStack in PrintMembers - @RikkiGibson
PR: Add generic attributes proposal - @RikkiGibson
PR: A meeting with lambda improvements mentioned - @WhiteBlackGoose
item.description: I was looking for the status of naturally typed lambdas and couldn't find. Then Fred gave me a link to a meeting which, I assume, should be mentioned in the lambda improvements proposal too, since it touches them.

PR: fix: special type implementation for array - @bernd5
PR: Update the interpolated string handler spec - @333fred
item.description:
Updates the spec to reflect the naming changes, the change in Create vs ctor, and the other open questions that have since been resolved.

@chsienki @AlekseyTs @stephentoub for review.

item.title: PR: Update "Unresolved questions" section in static-abstracts-in-interfaces.md - @AlekseyTs
PR: Split list patterns on enumerables - @jcouv
item.description: This is mostly a refactoring, with minor edits for readability. FYI @alrz

item.title: PR: Update list-patterns.md - @alrz
item.description:
Record recent LDM decisions and sync with the current impl.

Linking to test plan: https://github.com/dotnet/roslyn/issues/51289

item.title: PR: Fix typo - @YairHalberstadt
PR: Adjust 'is' spec - @RikkiGibson
PR: Clarify struct field initialization with default constructor initializer - @cston
item.description:
If a struct constructor calls : this() and the parameterless constructor is the default zero-init constructor, any fields initializers are executed in the calling constructor rather than the parameterless constructor.

Compare S1(int) and S2(int) below. Both call : this(). In S1, the parameterless constructor is the explicit, so S1.X is initialized in S1(). In S2, the parameterless constructor is the default zero-init constructor so S2.X is initialized in S2(int) instead.

struct S1
{
    int X = 1;
    int Y;
    public S1() { } // X = 1;
    public S1(int y) : this() { Y = y; }
}

struct S2
{
    int X = 2;
    int Y;
    public S2(int y) : this() { Y = y; } // X = 2;
}

item.title: PR: Propose rules for consuming user-defined conversion operators declared in interfaces. - @AlekseyTs
PR: Typo and rendering fixes - @jnm2
PR: Fix variable name - @Youssef1313
PR: Update LDM-2021-05-10.md - @deepumi
PR: Update lambda / method group natural type to use "function type" - @cston
item.description: Update the description of lambda and method group natural type based on a proposal from @MadsTorgersen of a "function type".

item.title: PR: Rename statics-in-interfaces.md to static-abstracts-in-interfaces.md - @RikkiGibson
item.description: When I went looking for the "static abstract members in interfaces" proposal, I was a little confused by the current file name, since static members are allowed in interfaces prior to this proposal. Please consider this alternate file name.

item.title: PR: Typos - @jnm2
PR: Rework improved interpolated string builder proposal with LDM feedback. - @333fred
item.description: This updates the interpolated string proposal with the latest ldm feedback, and gets it ready for the remaining open questions to be asked tomorrow.

item.title: PR: async is neccessary in Task Main entrypoint - @wuyuansushen
item.description: We need to explicitly use return <Task> statement to return a Task object without async modifier. It violates Task design principle (for asynchronous programing).

item.title: PR: Clarify description of inferred type for method group - @cston

PR: Improved definite assignment: accept more bool patterns - @RikkiGibson
item.description:
/cc @333fred related to the change to propagate conditional state through expr is not true etc patterns.

Related to dotnet/roslyn#52616

item.title: PR: Fix indentation - @alrz
PR: Update interpolated string proposal - @333fred
item.description: Updates the proposal with feedback from API review and previous LDM sessions.

item.title: PR: Update lambda improvements proposal - @cston
PR: Fix readonly property samples - @meziantou
item.description:
The original code doesn't compile. readonly can be set on a getter or setter when only one of them is readonly. If all setters are readonly, readonly must be set on the property.

As this file is available in the documentation, I think this is better to keep it up to date.

  • https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/language-reference/keywords/readonly#c-language-specification
  • https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/language-reference/proposals/csharp-8.0/readonly-instance-members

item.title: PR: Fix typo - @alrz
PR: Update improved-interpolated-strings.md - @deepumi

item.description: Proposed expansion of static abstract members in interfaces to support equality operators and user-defined conversions, as well ad default implementations

item.title: PR: Add Variance safety section to statics-in-interfaces.md - @AlekseyTs

item.description: The sample code won't compile. The indexer and properties need to be accessible.

item.title: PR: Use AsyncMethodBuilder and remove scoped scenarios - @jcouv
PR: Add pattern-variables.md - @alrz
PR: Update interpolated string proposal with feedback - @333fred
item.description:
• Split the TryFormat methods into TryFormatBaseString and TryFormatInterpolationHole. Naming is definitely subject to review, but hopefully the concept is clear.
  • Added new open questions for logging scenarios after review with the ILogger folks.
  • Added leeway for async rules in using the interpolated string builder for string types.
  • Added open question for disposal of builder types.

item.title: PR: Rename record equality parameters to 'left' and 'right' - @C-xC-c
PR: Address conversions of conditional accesses in IDA - @RikkiGibson
item.description: It feels like we can't propagate "state when not null" out of conditional accesses when they are subject to certain user-defined conversions, but it is desirable to propagate out such state from built-in implicit and explicit conversions whenever possible.

item.description:

item.title: PR: Minor clarifications to parameterless struct constructor proposal - @cston
PR: Remove mention of "copy constructor" - @jcouv
PR: Help to set expectations for issues up front - @jnm2
item.description:
From a discussion with @333fred, @CyrusNajmabadi, @MadsTorgersen, @YairHalberstadt, incorporating suggestions by Yair and Fred

The motivation is to close any gap between how the language design team already effectively works, and expectations of community members who want to create a new issue:

  • Avoiding a contributor feeling that moving to a discussion is a rejection of the time and level of quality put into their proposal. (The comprehensiveness is what the current directions emphasize.)
  • Directing contributors to the right spot in the first place.
  • Showing contributors that community discussions are the normal path to get a proposal that would be considered by the LDM, as opposed to thinking chances are increased by doing a big walk-up proposal, or thinking chances are lessened by being a "just" a discussion.

Wording suggestions welcome!

The changes to the titles and tag lines look like this:

item.title: PR: Spec determination of builder type from overrides - @jcouv
item.description:
LDM recommend that we try and simplify the module-level usage of overrides. This proposal explains how we determine the applicable override/builder type when the attribute doesn't specify a "target return type".

@stephentoub @dotnet/roslyn-compiler for review.

Relates to async override proposal: https://github.com/dotnet/csharplang/issues/1407
